LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Continued To Feb-24-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CARLSON, CALLADINE & PETERSON LLP AND J. MATTHEWS' MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Carlson, Calladine & Peterson, LLP and J. Michael Matthews' motion to strike Plaintiffs' prayer for punitive damages from the Second Amended Complaint is granted. This is a legal malpractice action in which Plaintiffs assert claims for professional negligence, breach of a written and oral contract, and breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. In order to state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, a complaint must set forth the elements stated in the general punitive damage statute, Civil Code section 3294, including allegations that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. (Civ. Code section 3294(a).) "Malice" is defined as conduct "intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others." (Civ. Codesection 3294(c)(1).) Absent an intent to injure the plaintiff, malice requires more than a "willful and conscious" disregard of the plaintiff's interests; the additional component of "despicable conduct" must be found. (College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 725.)

Here, the Second Amended Complaint is devoid of factual allegations sufficient to support the prayer for punitive damages. Smith v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1033 is controlling. (See id. at 1042 [issuing writ directing trial court to strike allegations and prayer for punitive damages in legal malpractice action where amended complaint was "devoid of any factual assertions supporting a conclusion [defendants] acted with oppression, fraud or malice"].) Neither Plaintiffs' allegation that Defendants engaged in overbilling for duplicative or unnecessary services nor their speculation that "discovery on these issues may uncover sufficient evidence to support an award of punitive damages" (Opp. at 4:1-2) is sufficient to support the prayer.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
